Thriller.description: Fast decay, high recovery needs, higher tolerance for intensity.

Drama.description: Slow decay, focuses on sustained emotional arcs.

Comedy.description: Medium decay, very fast recovery (jokes reset tension).

Horror.description: Very fast decay (jump scares), very slow recovery (dread).

psychological_thriller.description: Very slow decay — sustained dread accumulates deliberately. Very low recovery: the discomfort is the point.

romantic_comedy.description: Frequent comedic relief resets tension. Low fatigue threshold — audiences expect a light touch throughout.

dark_comedy.description: Moderate decay with moderate tonal whiplash expected. Balances discomfort with sardonic release.

period_drama.description: Very slow decay — audiences invest in long, deliberate emotional arcs. Archaic language readability reduced by era-language fix.

sci_fi.description: Moderate-slow decay. World-building exposition is expected and tolerated. Higher cognitive tolerance for density.

literary_drama.description: Very slow decay. Dense monologues and high idea density are intentional and tolerated by target audience.

war_film.description: Fast action-driven decay. High visual intensity throughout. Audience expects brutal peaks with tactical quiet valleys.

coming_of_age.description: Moderate-slow decay with generous recovery. Audiences are emotionally porous — low fatigue threshold for harsh demands.
